evals.prompt: Scaffold the eval system into a fresh pnpm monorepo with two skills under plugins/example-plugin/skills/.
evals.expected_output: Static pytest backend stamped at evals/, LLM backend at evals/_llm/, evals.json generated for each skill with _meta.generated=true, manifest.yml and manifest.history.yml written, and package.json merged with eval scripts. pnpm run eval:update --check exits 0.
assertions: evals/conftest.py exists
evals/_llm/runner.ts exists
Each discovered skill has evals/evals.json with >= 2 cases
Every generated case includes _meta.generated = true
Every generated case includes a 64-character sha256 source_hash in _meta
package.json includes the eval, eval:full, eval:update, eval:update:check and eval:compare scripts
.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml exists and validates against manifest.schema.json
.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml contains exactly one snapshot after create
pnpm run eval:update --check exits with code 0

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-create in a repository that already has a user-authored case at skills/foo/evals/evals.json (no _meta block). Ensure the user case is preserved.
evals.expected_output: The user-authored case is untouched; new generated cases may be appended, but existing cases without _meta remain byte-for-byte identical. All interactive prompts go through askQuestion.
assertions: Pre-existing user-authored case is preserved verbatim (no _meta added retroactively)
Any new generated cases have _meta.generated = true
The skill uses askQuestion before overwriting or appending to any existing evals.json
.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml lists the user's evals.json as an eval_file for the relevant target

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-create in a repository whose git working tree has no HEAD (brand new repo with no initial commit).
evals.expected_output: The skill completes successfully and records a placeholder git_ref in the manifest, clearly flagged. No crash.
assertions: The skill does not crash on missing HEAD
manifest.yml is written with a placeholder or zero sha git_ref
The skill asks, via askQuestion, whether the user wants to make an initial commit before continuing

evals.prompt: `/z-eval-create` already routed here, but this clone still lacks `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`. Run the **zoto-create-evals** workflow you would normally perform after approvals and tell me exactly what comes back before any scaffolding executes.
assertions: The visible result is `needs_user_input` that tells the operator to run `/z-eval-configure` (or equivalent) before scaffolding, matching Step 1.
No `askQuestion` tool calls originate from this skill workflow.
Nothing new appears under `{evalsDir}/`, no `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml`, and no `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml` append occurs because configuration was missing.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill scaffolds dual static and LLM eval backends, reconciles discovery output with pre-approved targets and ignore minimatches, stamps central command, agent, and hook JSON via analyse and stamp while templating most skills, merges package scripts and devDependencies, writes manifests with append-only history, and finishes with non-interactive gates and hygiene notes.

evals.expected_output: A single `needs_user_input` hand-off that names configure-first work, with no filesystem mutations under the eval directory or manifest paths.

evals.prompt: Host agent continuation after `/z-eval-create` finished its checklists: approved ids are `command:z-eval-init`, `agent:zoto-eval-generator`, `hook:cursor-workspace`, `skill:zoto-create-evals`, and `skill:zoto-eval-tooling`. Load **zoto-create-evals**, treat that approved list as authoritative against `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, run the full workflow through validation, and return the final report without issuing interactive questions from inside the skill itself.
assertions: Before template copies, an explore subagent runs `pnpm run eval:discover` and the retained targets align with Step 3’s authoritative approved id list filtered by `config.ignore` minimatches.

Excluded ids never trigger `pnpm run eval:analyse -- …` nor `pnpm run eval:stamp -- …`; if invoked out of band, tooling would exit cleanly with `{ ignored: true, matched_glob }` semantics rather than partial writes.

Both backends are always stamped: `templates/static/pytest/*` under `{evalsDir}/` and `templates/llm/agent-sdk/*` under `{evalsDir}/_llm/`, plus `templates/runner/test.py.tmpl` → `scripts/test.py` and `templates/schema/result.schema.json` beside the LLM harness.

Approved skills receive `templates/skill-evals/evals.json.tmpl` overlays; generic `skill:*` rows are not centrally stamped via `eval-stamp.ts`, while `skill:zoto-eval-tooling` additionally goes through `pnpm run eval:analyse -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling` and `pnpm run eval:stamp -- skill:zoto-eval-tooling` per `CENTRAL_STAMP_SKILL_ALLOWLIST`.

For each surviving central target, stamped plugin and workspace mirrors follow the documented table (for example `.cursor/evals/commands/...`, `.cursor/evals/agents/...`, and `.cursor/evals/hooks/hooks.json` for `hook:cursor-workspace`) and emitted JSON retains `fixtures` plus `expected_filesystem` payloads suitable for realism.
New hook work targets `hook:cursor-workspace`; if the deprecated `hook:cursor` alias appears, tooling may warn but canonical ids stay on the workspace bundle.
`static.framework`, `llm.strategy`, and `llm.codeFramework` values come straight from `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` so framework selection never silently drifts when operators flip backends.

`pnpm exec tsx scripts/package-json-merger.ts` merges `templates/package-scripts/base.json` idempotently, adds `dotenv` among devDependencies without clobbering existing versions unless forced, and the operator-facing report reminds them to run an install (`pnpm`, `yarn`, or `npm`) once.

`.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` carries `schema_version`, timestamps, `git_ref`, `generated_by: zoto-create-evals`, discovery configuration, and every target’s `eval_files`; the identical YAML snapshot is appended to `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml` without rewriting prior history.

Every generated eval row includes `_meta.generated: true`, `source_hash`, `primitive_analysis_hash`, ISO `last_updated`, and `generated_by: "zoto-create-evals"`, optionally `partial` when only shape-level signal existed.
Validation gates succeed: `pnpm run eval:list`, `pnpm run eval -- --collect-only`, and `pnpm run eval:update --check` all exit 0.

The closing report reminds operators they may run `pnpm exec tsx scripts/eval-cleanup-vendored.ts` after tightening `ignore[]` to drop generated-only vendored fixtures, and notes that mixed user-authored and generated assets require `needs_user_input` instead of silent deletes.
No application or library sources outside the documented allow-list are edited, and this skill never calls `askQuestion`.
No `.env` file is created anywhere in the repository by this workflow.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill scaffolds dual static and LLM eval backends, reconciles discovery output with pre-approved targets and ignore minimatches, stamps central command, agent, and hook JSON via analyse and stamp while templating most skills, merges package scripts and devDependencies, writes manifests with append-only history, and finishes with non-interactive gates and hygiene notes.

evals.expected_output: Both backends land under the configured eval directory, central JSON exists for each surviving command, agent, and hook target, `skill:zoto-eval-tooling` picked up stamped rows while other skills keep templated `evals/evals.json`, manifests and history updated together, `package.json` gained eval automation without touching unrelated sources, validators pass, root credentials template handling is spelled out, and no `.env` file is introduced.

evals.prompt: Continue **zoto-create-evals** on a workspace whose `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` already lists `ignore: ['vendor/**']`. The Task-approved ids include `command:z-eval-init` plus a second command id whose canonical markdown path normalises to `vendor/acme-cli/commands/extra.md`. Show how you sequence discovery, filtering, and stamping so the vendor-backed id is dropped before any `eval:analyse` / `eval:stamp` calls while the clean id still produces central JSON.
assertions: After mapping ids to canonical sources, any path matching `vendor/**` is removed before dispatching `pnpm run eval:analyse --` or `pnpm run eval:stamp --`, so no new central JSON appears for that vendor-scoped command.
The retained `command:z-eval-init` still receives the full analyse-and-stamp sequence described in Step 4a.
The final narrative references post-`ignore` hygiene via `pnpm exec tsx scripts/eval-cleanup-vendored.ts` so legacy generated-only assets under newly ignored trees can be purged safely.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill scaffolds dual static and LLM eval backends, reconciles discovery output with pre-approved targets and ignore minimatches, stamps central command, agent, and hook JSON via analyse and stamp while templating most skills, merges package scripts and devDependencies, writes manifests with append-only history, and finishes with non-interactive gates and hygiene notes.
fixture_justifications: Provides a concrete `ignore` minimatch so Step 3 filtering and the documented `{ ignored: true, matched_glob }` contract can be exercised without relying on implicit defaults.

files.content:
schema_version: 1
evalsDir: evals
ignore:
  - "vendor/**"
static:
  framework: pytest
llm:
  strategy: code
  codeFramework: vitest

evals.expected_output: Report proves the vendor-scoped command never receives central tooling, matching glob output is acknowledged, and the surviving command still ends with stamped eval JSON in both plugin and workspace trees.

evals.prompt: Run **zoto-create-evals** after `/z-eval-create` approvals, using the existing `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` in this workspace where manual operator checklists are turned on. Ensure the user-facing checklist artifact lands beside the static eval tree as documented.
assertions: When `config.manualChecklists.enabled` is true, `templates/user-checklists/USER_EVAL_CHECKLISTS.md.tmpl` is copied to `{evalsDir}/USER_EVAL_CHECKLISTS.md` verbatim aside from normative path rewrites.
No `askQuestion` calls belong to this skill even when extra files are emitted.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill scaffolds dual static and LLM eval backends, reconciles discovery output with pre-approved targets and ignore minimatches, stamps central command, agent, and hook JSON via analyse and stamp while templating most skills, merges package scripts and devDependencies, writes manifests with append-only history, and finishes with non-interactive gates and hygiene notes.
fixture_justifications: Forces the Step 6 branch that copies `templates/user-checklists/USER_EVAL_CHECKLISTS.md.tmpl` into `{evalsDir}/USER_EVAL_CHECKLISTS.md` so the behaviour is observable.

files.content:
schema_version: 1
evalsDir: evals
ignore: []
manualChecklists:
  enabled: true
static:
  framework: pytest
llm:
  strategy: code
  codeFramework: vitest

evals.expected_output: `USER_EVAL_CHECKLISTS.md` appears under `{evalsDir}/` because `manualChecklists.enabled` is true, while the rest of the scaffold still follows the standard dual-backend path.

evals.prompt: Execute the approved **zoto-create-evals** pass on a repository that already contains a hand-edited root env template with a unique marker line. Confirm the skill reproduces the dual-backend scaffold while respecting the non-destructive env-template rule and still steering operators toward local credentials.
assertions: The workspace copy of `.env.example` still contains `ZOTO_EVAL_LOCAL_MARKER=create-evals-respect-existing-file` after the run, proving the file was not overwritten.
The closing report includes a one-line note reminding operators to confirm the `CURSOR_API_KEY` entry without rewriting the file.
No `.env` file is created, matching the explicit prohibition.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill scaffolds dual static and LLM eval backends, reconciles discovery output with pre-approved targets and ignore minimatches, stamps central command, agent, and hook JSON via analyse and stamp while templating most skills, merges package scripts and devDependencies, writes manifests with append-only history, and finishes with non-interactive gates and hygiene notes.
fixture_justifications: Creates the Step 4 branch where `.env.example` already exists so the skill must skip overwriting and instead surface the documented confirmation note in the final report.

files.content:
# Operator-specific secrets stay out of git
CURSOR_API_KEY=
ZOTO_EVAL_LOCAL_MARKER=create-evals-respect-existing-file

evals.expected_output: Scaffolding completes, but the pre-existing root env template file remains byte-stable while the report adds a one-line reminder to verify `CURSOR_API_KEY` coverage; no `.env` file is written.
